name: The 13th Warrior
comment: The dog is seen at the end of the movie safe.

name: This Is the End
comment: Near the beginning of the film we see a photo of a pet dog and are told it is incontinent. Near the end of the film the dog is very briefly seen (with no signs of incontinence). Arguments could be made for giving the film a :'-( but since nothing bad happe

name: Maurice
comment: a few wild rabbits are hunted, and one audibly has its neck snapped.

name: Suspicion
comment: There is a wonderful dog, but he is unharmed.

name: City Slickers
comment: Two horses die and their graves are shown.

name: District 9
comment: The aliens primarily eat raw meat and there are many cow carcasses on screen as a result. Body parts of pigs and sheep are also shown and mutilated in various ways, but no animal (beside maybe the sentiant aliens, depending on your definition of 'animal') is actively killed on screen. All are already dead before they are on screen.

name: Insidious
comment: There are no animals in this movie

name: Ginger Snaps
comment: *spoilers***   The scenes with the dogs look real fake.  I hate animal death scenes especially the yelping. But this is one of my favorite movies.  Yes theres a part where they show a dead dog and it looks really fake. There is one part where Ginger kicks the rottwelier but its very quick and its because he was barking at her because he knew she wasn't herself.  Its not graphic and it's a very very quick Yelp I barely heard it.   There is a part where the sisters find a dead dog and try moving it and one of its legs comes off.. again looks really fake.  If you're extremely sensitive to even the slightest amount of gore with a dog.. then yes.. stay away but its not that bad.  Ginger does eat the neighbors dog but it does not show in scene only the aftermath where she's throwing up the blood.

name: The Gold Rush
comment: A bear is shot and eaten off screen.

name: August: Osage County
comment: A story is told about pet parakeets dying from the heat. Since the birds are only mentioned and never seen, we're not counting them as actual deaths.

name: Hook
comment: Nana, a sheepdog, shows up in a few scenes and is not harmed.

name: La La Land
comment: A lighthearted romp where neither animals nor people are in danger.

name: [REC]
comment: the health inspector suggests that the little girl's dog got a bunch of other animals infected. it's only said not seen

name: Possessor
comment: In the uncut version (on Hulu Oct 2023) there is a flash of a cat laying in a pool of blood

name: The Wolf of Wall Street
comment: Someone eats a fish alive

name: Hot Rod
comment: A raccoon is run over, it isn’t shown but it’s directly bragged about and you hear it and see the car jump :(

name: Watchmen
comment: Ozymandias’ genetically altered cat is disintergrated

name: Octopussy
comment: Bond kills a spider, and possibly a leech.

name: 4 Months, 3 Weeks and 2 Days
comment: No. There are two kittens in this movie and they are safe! It seems several people have been trolling by voting yes for everything for some reason.

name: Hannibal
comment: A dead pigeon is shown.

name: Ratatouille
comment: Dead rats are briefly shown hanging from mousetraps in a store window.

name: Coming to America
comment: In the room they rent in queens the outline of a dog is on the floor that was clearly killed (you don’t actually see the dog) and the man says “damn shame what they did to that dog”

name: Homeward Bound: the Incredible Journey
comment: Two dogs and a cat end up in perilous situations while trying to get home. There are moments when it appears one or another of the animals has died, but in the end they all survive.

name: Dune: Part Two
comment: No dogs in this movie

name: National Lampoon's Christmas Vacation
comment: A cat dies from being electricuted. It's extremely upsetting, especially because everyone just laughs rather than recognizing the cat's death. Awful

name: Contagion
comment: Monkeys and pigs.  You don't see them die, you just see them alive, then dead.

name: Midnight Express
comment: A cat gets hanged.

name: Out of Africa
comment: There is hunting at the beginning of the film and a bird is shot out of the sky. An ox is attacked by a lion, which begins eating it alive until they chase it off. The ox's death is not shown but it moans in pain and presumably dies. Two lions are shot.

name: The Beastmaster
comment: One of the ferrets dies by saving Dar from Maax. Pretty sad death, but just know that the ferret is a good actor.

name: The Whole Truth
comment: The cat is accidentally poisoned but what is shown is a high quality photo of a cat just resting with a pool of fake blood. The above comment was unwarranted and xenophobic.

name: The Addams Family
comment: Mentioned but not shown

name: Escape Room
comment: No live animals are shown in the film. There are some models ( which might be stuffed dead ones ) and some mounted deer antlers.

name: The Lorax
comment: They don't die, but are sent to live elsewhere once their home is polluted.

name: Enchanted
comment: A cockroach is eaten by a pigeon

name: Escape From Alcatraz
comment:
The protagonist, Frank Morris, scoops up a deceased friend's pet mouse to take on the escape. 

He puts the mouse in his trouser pocket and then later wades chest high into the water. The mouse is never seen again. 

There is also a rat that is thrown down a vent shaft.

name: The Beguiled
comment: These comments belong under "Does an Animal Die" not dogs. A turtle is obviously not a dog.

name: Osmosis Jones
comment: No, but Frank does try to choke a Monkey for stealing his egg early on in the movie (He fails spectacularly)

name: Evil Dead
comment: Yes, the main character kills the dog. Unnecessary.

name: Horns
comment: Didn't find a seperate listing for the book. In chapter 37 a cat is murdered.

name: The Mummy
comment: No cats are harmed. The white cat used to scare the mummy is fine.

name: Serpico
comment: The dog stays besides him till the end.

name: Dawn of the Planet of the Apes
comment: While hunting, the apes move in on a deer they injured and it looks like they are about to kill it. Out of nowhere, a bear jumps out and attacks the apes. They fight back and eventually kill the bear. While its not clear, the deer probably got away.

name: Flight of the Navigator
comment: Amazingly, the boy disappears for eight years and when he comes back the dog is still alive! Bravo!

name: Mousehunt
comment: Sort of. You don't actually see anything, to be honest I'm not sure if the cat even dies, but it seems to be implied?

name: Chungking Express
comment: someone is shot while holding kittens but none die

name: Get Out
comment: No, I lived; thank you. 9/9 lives => Would recommend.

name: A Little Princess
comment: During the main character's fictional story she tells throughout the movie, a deer dies to give life back to the prince who was poisoned in the story. There is no blood or violence, the deer lays down next to him and it is simply said that "she gave her life" to him by the narrator.

name: The People Under the Stairs
comment: An attack dog is electrocuted and injured while chasing trespassers then killed when its owner accidentally stabs it through a wall with a machete.

name: A Boy Called Christmas
comment: Towards the beginning, an ax slams down and chops a small piece of apple in half directly in front of the mouse. Personally, I was triggered because I thought it was the mouse. But, it was only a piece of apple.

name: The Age of Adaline
comment: Her dog (her closest companion in the first part of the film) dies from old age related conditions about half way through the film.
